a:r.a:t: Before we dive into the specifics of our system, let's establish what we mean by an "agent harness." In the AI world, an LLM by itself is just a text prediction engine -- it takes text in, produces text out, and that's it. An agent harness is the orchestration framework that wraps around the LLM to give it actual capabilities: tool access, memory, multi-step reasoning, and task management. Think of it like the difference between a brain in a jar and a brain connected to hands, eyes, and a nervous system. The harness handles the reasoning loop (observe, think, act, repeat), manages state between turns, routes requests to specialized agents, and enforces security policies on what tools each agent can use. Every major production AI system -- from coding assistants to enterprise chatbots -- uses some form of agent harness. Ours is built on LangChain/LangGraph with custom extensions for multi-agent coordination, knowledge graph integration, and enterprise access control. The next few slides break down why this architecture matters and how the core patterns work.
